doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document does not correspond to any verifiable HUD or Illinois Attorney General publication, and its core legal claim — that a 5-day pay-or-quit notice obligates a tenant to vacate without a right to cure or a court proceeding — is a da
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 96
REASON: This document contains critically false legal information — a 5-day pay-or-quit notice in Illinois does *not* require a tenant to vacate; it gives the tenant the right to cure by paying rent or contest the matter in court, and a landlord must still file an eviction lawsuit to obtain possession — and no such jointly-issued HUD/Illinois AG document (TRF-2026-IL) exists in any verifiable HUD or Illinois AG publication series, making this a fabricated document that dangerously misrepresents tenant rights.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 96
REASON: The document does not correspond to any verifiable HUD or Illinois Attorney General publication, and its core legal claim — that a 5-day pay-or-quit notice obligates a tenant to vacate without a right to cure or a court proceeding — is a dangerous misrepresentation of Illinois landlord-tenant law.
